title: Pergularia roylei
taxonomy: Pergularia roylei is classified under the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Pergularia is a monotypic genus, containing only one species, Pergularia roylei. Pergularia roylei is native to the Himalayan region and is often found in dry forests and scrublands. The plant is further classified in the order Gentianales and the phylum Angiosperms.
uses: Pergularia roylei is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help in reducing fever and treating skin infections. It is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. In addition, Pergularia roylei is thought to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it useful for wound healing. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Pergularia roylei for medicinal purposes.

intro: Pergularia roylei, also known as Satyriasis root or Anwala, is a medicinal plant native to the Indian subcontinent, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of erectile dysfunction, and its ability to boost fertility and libido in both men and women. The bioactive constituents of Pergularia roylei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pergularia roylei is commonly used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine, and is often prepared in the form of decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions.
